signals.reasoning: BTC is still holding near the top of the recovery range with trend-supported price plus open-interest behavior, but the board is under active VETO from fresh relief-style geopolitical headlines, so this is watch-only instead of a chase long.
ETH still has constructive upside structure and cleaner major-relative strength than most of the board, but the active VETO makes even the better-looking majors non-actionable here.
SOL remains structurally firm after the squeeze and is not breaking down cleanly enough for a fresh short, while the event brake argues against forcing a long after the rebound.
GRIFFAIN is already deep into a long-liquidation flush with heavy negative funding, so it is too extended to chase short and too damaged to trust as a fresh long under VETO.
CHILLGUY looks more like a short-covering rally than a clean fresh trend, which makes it a poor chase long and not a clean short while the event regime stays headline-driven.
POLYX has the best raw squeeze fuel on the sheet with deeply negative funding, but it is thin, already stretched, and exactly the kind of setup the live VETO is meant to keep as watch-only.
LDO still shows constructive trend-supported upside, but the move is already extended and the event brake keeps it below the bar for a fresh signal.
ZEREBRO has recovered constructively, but the setup still looks like noisy continuation rather than a clean new entry, so it stays watch-only under VETO.
XPL is strong but incomplete on the mechanical side and already well off the lows, so the combination of extension plus VETO keeps it non-actionable.
KNEIRO has strong negative-funding squeeze fuel, but the data is incomplete and the move is already extended enough that under active VETO it belongs on the watchlist, not as a live trade.
